screenshot_note: Puppeteer failed — pthread_create: Resource temporarily unavailable (system thread limit hit). Review conducted on source code alone. Screenshots from luna-91 (ErrorBoundary crash) and sofia-81 (post-Carlos fix) remain the most recent visual references. Three confirmed fixes verified by reading App.jsx and Office.jsx directly.
theme: Three must-fixes landed silently between reviews — the stale closure is gone, the radiogroups finally behave like radiogroups, and the ErrorBoundary can now recover. The office has better bones. Now let's fix the cracks that opened while we were patching the walls.

fixed_since_last_review.title: handleTabKeyDown Stale Closure — FIXED (Sofia Strike 4 Resolved)
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx:714-716 + 803-816 — const tabRef = useRef(tab) and useEffect(() => { tabRef.current = tab }, [tab]) added. handleTabKeyDown now reads tabRef.current inside the callback and has empty dep array []. Zero stale-closure risk, zero unnecessary tab-button re-renders. Exactly the fix proposed in review 92. Four QA reviews + one creative review to land a 10-line change. It's here now.

fixed_since_last_review.title: ARIA Radiogroup Filter Bars — Arrow-Key Navigation and Roving TabIndex — FIXED (Sofia Strike 3 Resolved)
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx:1389-1456 — Both activity filter radiogroups now have: (1) onKeyDown on the container handling ArrowLeft/ArrowRight with setActivityFilter/setWorkerFilter, (2) tabIndex={activityFilter === key ? 0 : -1} on each type-filter radio, (3) tabIndex={workerFilter === member.name ? 0 : -1} on each worker radio. Roving tabindex pattern is correct. Keyboard users can now navigate both filter bars with arrow keys as WCAG requires.

fixed_since_last_review.title: ErrorBoundary: componentDidCatch + Retry Button — FIXED
fixed_since_last_review.how_fixed: App.jsx:12-19 — componentDidCatch(error, info) { console.error('[Bloberto ErrorBoundary]', error, info) } is present. resetError = () => this.setState({ hasError: false }) is present. Retry button rendered inline with the error message. The 48-minute blind diagnosis window from the March 11 crash is closed. The next incident will leave a forensic record and give users a one-click recovery path.

ideas.title: WorkerCard Back Panel Key Uses Delimiter-Free Concatenation — The Same Bug We Fixed in ActivityLogEntry
ideas.description: App.jsx:395 — workerHistory.map((e) => (<div key={e.timestamp + e.type + (e.worker || '')}...)). Review 92 fixed the ActivityLogEntry key (now uses underscore-separated template literal at line 157). The WorkerCard back panel was missed. It still uses the pre-fix string concatenation: if two events have the same timestamp and if that timestamp string ends with a character that equals the start of e.type, keys collide and React silently reorders or drops entries. The probability is low but non-zero — ISO timestamp ending in 'z' + type starting with 'z' (never happens) or a system event where worker is empty and type is 'error' after a timestamp ending in 'e'. More importantly: inconsistency. The ActivityLogEntry was fixed; the WorkerCard back panel was not. A future dev reading both will wonder why one uses delimiters and one doesn't. Fix: key={`${e.timestamp}_${e.type}_${e.worker ?? 'system'}`} — identical pattern to the fix that landed in review 92. Two-character change. WHY: the fix was applied in half the codebase. Finish it.

ideas.title: standupWorkers Reference Equality Split — Partial Fix Created Inconsistent Behavior
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1443 vs 1571 — Two standup checks exist in the same render, now using different strategies. Line 1443: const isStandup = occ && standupWorkers.some(sw => sw.id === occ.worker.id) — uses .some(), id-safe. Line 1571: if (standupWorkers.includes(occ.worker)) return null — still uses reference equality .includes(). This is WORSE than review 92's original single-check problem. Before the partial fix, both code paths used .includes() — consistently wrong. Now: the CSS class check (line 1443) uses .some() while the Character render skip (line 1571) uses .includes(). If references ever diverge (normalization clones, spread merge, any new worker field added during fetch), desk CSS will correctly exclude the standup class BUT the Character will still render at the desk — producing a ghost duplicate: the worker appears standing at their desk AND at the conference table simultaneously, with no thrown error and no obvious cause. The desk CSS says standup; the desk character says I'm here. Fix: change line 1571 to if (standupWorkers.some(sw => sw.id === occ.worker.id)) return null — one method call change. WHY: the partial fix made the failure mode harder to detect by creating an inconsistency between two paths that should always agree.

ideas.title: ConferenceTable SVG Filter Has Hardcoded ID — Document-Scope Collision Risk
ideas.description: Office.jsx:941 — <filter id="conf-fire-glow" ...>. SVG filter IDs are document-scoped. ConferenceTable is currently rendered once, so no collision today. But: if kiosk mode (from review 92 wild idea) is ever built, if a test renders ConferenceTable twice, or if a future layout needs two office views side-by-side, the second instance's SVG will silently reference the first instance's filter definition — or not apply any glow at all depending on DOM parse order. CharacterAvatar already solves this correctly at line 230: const gradId = useId() — the pattern is established in this codebase. Fix: add const glowFilterId = useId() at the top of ConferenceTable, replace id="conf-fire-glow" with id={glowFilterId}, and update the url() reference to url(#${glowFilterId}). Three lines. WHY: CharacterAvatar knows why global SVG IDs are dangerous. ConferenceTable should know too.

ideas.title: Window Sky Gradient and Header Greeting Are Two Hours Out of Sync
ideas.description: App.jsx:866-871 (WindowElement) vs App.jsx:1163 — The window sky gradient activates deep purple night (linear-gradient(#4c1d95, #312e81)) at h >= 20 (8pm). The header greeting switches from 'Good evening' to 'Burning the midnight oil' at h >= 22 (10pm, the isAfterHours threshold fixed in review 91). The vibe system likely activates after-hours at a similar late threshold. Result: at 8pm and 9pm, the office window looks like deep midnight, starless purple sky — but the header cheerfully says 'Good evening, boss' and the team vibe doesn't feel after-hours at all. The visual storytelling and the verbal storytelling disagree for two full hours every evening. Fix: extend the golden-hour sky gradient to cover h >= 17 && h <= 21, and start the night-purple sky at h >= 22 to match the isAfterHours boundary. This aligns the window with the greeting and the vibe — the office looks late when it feels late. WHY: the sky is the most visible ambient storytelling element in the office view. When it says 'midnight' while the header says 'evening,' the immersion cracks.

ideas.title: Luna's Creative Reviews Are Ghost Events — Never Appear in the Activity Log (Carry-Over Review 10)
ideas.description: data/activity.json — Luna has completed 93 creative reviews. Zero appear in the activity log. The feed shows Sofia QA reviews and Carlos fixes and system vibe transitions. Luna does not exist in her own team's record. This has been flagged in reviews 83 through 93 — ten consecutive reviews. The action: after writing ideas.json, Luna appends an entry to activity.json with type='complete', worker='Luna', message='Creative review #93 complete — N ideas, N fixes verified', timestamp=ISO now. Same pattern Sofia uses after each QA review. This is not a code change. It is a data maintenance action that takes sixty seconds. WHY: if you check the activity log to understand what the team has been doing for the past week, you will see QA and DevOps work. You will not see creative direction. Luna is the Creative Director of a product with no creative record. That is the tenth review in a row with the same sentence.

ideas.title: staleMins Computed Twice in the Same Render Block — Still Open from Review 92
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1433-1441 — Two separate Math.floor(staleDiffMs / 60000) computations exist 8 lines apart with different variable names: 'mins' inside the staleDeskTitle IIFE and 'ghostMins' in the tier-classification block. Review 92 flagged this and noted it as a clean-up of the partial fix from review 91. It is still open. The fix: const staleMins = isStaleWorking ? Math.floor(staleDiffMs / 60000) : 0 computed once before both uses; replace 'mins' and 'ghostMins' with staleMins throughout. One variable, one computation, no ambiguity about whether they could differ (they cannot — both compute the floor of the same staleDiffMs value). WHY: two different names for the same value in the same 20-line block implies to every reader that they might mean different things. They never can.

ideas.title: handleCharClick Over-Specified Dependency Array — Stable Values Listed as Dependencies
ideas.description: Office.jsx:1257 — }, [setPingedId, setPingReaction, pingTimerRef, pingReactionTimerRef, onWorkerClick]). Four of the five items in this dep array are stable by React contract: setPingedId and setPingReaction are useState setters (React guarantees stable references across renders); pingTimerRef and pingReactionTimerRef are useRef objects (same object reference for the component lifetime). Listing stable values in useCallback deps is not incorrect — React ignores values that never change — but it sends a misleading signal to every reader: 'this callback might recreate when setPingedId changes.' It never does. It also hides the one dep that matters (onWorkerClick, a prop that can genuinely change). Fix: }, [onWorkerClick]) — remove the four stable deps. WHY: dependency arrays should be documentation. An array of four stable values plus one real dep documents nothing useful and obscures the one thing worth knowing.

ideas.title: ETA Badge Shows Stale Predictions Without Decay — '~2m left' After 45 Minutes at 95%
ideas.description: App.jsx:235 — const eta = (progress > 5 && progress < 100 && workingMins > 1) ? Math.round((workingMins / progress) * (100 - progress)) : null. The linear extrapolation is sound when a task is moving. But when a worker is stuck at 95% progress and updated_at stopped changing 45 minutes ago, the ETA computes (workingMins / 95) * 5 — which at 46 working minutes returns ~2.4 minutes. The badge says '~2m left' while the worker's duration badge says 'Working for 46m.' The WhitBoard shows 95%. The contradiction is visible on the same card. A worker who has been at 95% for longer than their estimated remaining time should not show a confident ETA — they should show '⏱ overdue' or the ETA should be suppressed. Fix: add a stale check — if the worker's updated_at timestamp is older than eta * 60 * 1.5, render null instead of the ETA, or render a small '⏱ overdue' label. Check worker.updated_at vs Date.now() with a 1.5× eta multiplier. WHY: false precision is worse than no precision. The ETA is the most visible metric on the card and currently lies to the viewer's face during exactly the situation when they most want accurate information.

ideas.title: idleBubble Selection Is a 90-Character Inline Expression That Calls Date.now() in JSX
ideas.description: App.jsx:813 — The idle bubble emoji is selected by an inline expression inside JSX: (WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES[worker.id] || DEFAULT_IDLE_BUBBLES)[Math.floor((Date.now() / 12000) + (worker.id ? [...worker.id].reduce((h, c) => h * 31 + c.charCodeAt(0), 0) : 0)) % (WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES[worker.id] || DEFAULT_IDLE_BUBBLES).length]. Three problems: (1) Date.now() is called inline on every render — if the component re-renders even once in the same 12-second window, the expression is recomputed but should return the same value (it will, because Math.floor(Date.now() / 12000) rounds to a 12-second bucket) — so no correctness issue but cognitive cost is high. (2) The array is computed inline twice (once for the pool, once for .length) — same allocation path evaluated twice. (3) This is unreadable. The bubble has already been set by the setIdleBubble(true) call in the useEffect at line 604 — the displayed value should be determined at set time, not at render time. Fix: change the setIdleBubble(true) call to setIdleBubble(pickIdleBubble(worker.id)) where pickIdleBubble is a pure function above the component. Store the emoji string in state, not just a boolean. Render {idleBubble} directly. WHY: the delight of idle micro-bubbles deserves to be implemented in a way that can be read, tested, and changed without a 90-character archaeology dig.

ideas.title: quoteBonus Wrap Still Has No Ceremony — Ninth Consecutive Review
ideas.description: App.jsx:1471 — setQuoteBonus(p => p >= BONUS_TAGLINES.length ? 1 : p + 1). The tagline counter reaches 8/8 and resets to 1 with zero acknowledgment. This has been flagged in reviews 83, 84, 85, 86, 87, 88, 89, 90, 91, 92 — ten consecutive reviews including this one. Ninth creative review. The fix has been written in this file three times. The infrastructure is all present: safeSave for localStorage flags, completionToast state for the golden border, sparkle class system for the footer sparkle. The missing branch: when p >= BONUS_TAGLINES.length, instead of resetting to 1, call safeSave('footer-all-taglines-seen', '1'), setCompletionToast({ text: 'You found all of Bloberto’s thoughts. All eight of them.', color: '#fbbf24', type: 'milestone' }), add a footer-sparkle--complete class to freeze the sparkle gold, and THEN reset the counter. Maximum 20-line change. The wires are present. The spec has been written. Someone connect them. WHY: nine reviews. The tagline hunt is the only active discovery mechanic we have. A counter that goes 7/8 → silence is the worst possible ending to our only Easter egg. At some point 'this keeps being deprioritized' becomes 'this is the thing we never shipped' and that is the most embarrassing line on the creative record.

ideas.title: CSS ::after 'TRIPLE SHOT' Is Invisible to Screen Readers and Ctrl+F — Carry-Over from Review 92
ideas.description: Office.css — [data-vibe='crushing'] .coffee-corner__label::after { content: ' TRIPLE SHOT'; font-weight: 700; }. The crushing-vibe coffee corner label is injected as CSS pseudo-element content. Delightful detail — but CSS-generated content is not read by all screen readers (NVDA+Chrome skips it), cannot be selected, copied, or found by Ctrl+F. Fix: render TRIPLE SHOT in JSX conditionally when vibe === 'crushing', remove the ::after rule, add aria-label='Coffee — triple shot' on the corner container for that vibe. Same visual, correct semantics. WHY: visible content should live in the DOM. Carry-over from review 92.

_still_open_from_prior.note: Ninth consecutive creative review (83 through 93). Ten reviews total including QA flagging. Escalated to wild in review 93. The infrastructure is complete. This is a will problem.
Carry-over from review 92 (partial fix of review 91). Office.jsx:1433-1441. Two Math.floor(staleDiffMs / 60000) computations with different names.
CSS check at Office.jsx:1443 now uses .some(). Character render skip at Office.jsx:1571 still uses .includes(). Two checks now disagree on strategy — escalated to must in review 93.
Ten consecutive reviews. Luna has logged 93 creative reviews. Zero appear in activity.json. Sixty-second data entry task.
Carry-over from review 92. Coffee corner TRIPLE SHOT injected via CSS pseudo-element, invisible to screen readers.
Carry-over from review 92. workers.json entries missing role and id fields. Desks render with undefined role color.
